no_changes_rationale:
status: needs_investigation
reason: Bead ddx-5d49b14e is an epic that fails the size check decisively — six acceptance criteria span unrelated subsystems (technical design doc, backend-interface refactor, new axon backend implementation, migration tool, chaos-test integration, fallback documentation, performance benchmarking). The prior attempt produced only an execution result.json artifact and was BLOCKED in post-merge review for that reason. Per the execute-bead instructions, when a bead is too big the correct action is to decompose, not to attempt the work.

Decomposed into seven children (DAG):

  ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration for bead tracker
  ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface in cli/internal/bead     (depends on ddx-6bb51a52)
  ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker                              (depends on ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-cb63cdfc  Migration tool: .ddx/beads.jsonl + archive → axon backend            (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-743bc194  Run chaos_test.go against axon backend                              (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-8835a765  Document bd/doltdb fallback backend wiring                          (depends on ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-6602559c  Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive scale (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)

Each child is independently sized for one execute-bead pass with concrete, verifiable ACs. The parent epic ddx-5d49b14e should remain open as the rollup; close it once all seven children land and post-merge review passes on each.

Follow-up note for triage: the lock-contention v1 patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains the stability scaffolding while this epic plays out — it is not blocked by this decomposition.
